title: Beam management for deactivated secondary cell group (scg)
abstract: Embodiments include methods for a user equipment (UE) configured to communicate with a wireless network via a master cell group (MCG) and a secondary cell group (SCG). Such methods include entering a reduced-energy mode for the SCG responsive to receiving a first command via the MCG or the SCG. Such methods also include, while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for the MCG, performing SCG measurements and reporting the SCG measurements to the wireless network (e.g., via MCG or SCG). Other embodiments include complementary methods for first and second network nodes arranged, respectively, to provide the MCG and the SCG, as well as UEs and network nodes configured to perform the exemplary methods.Figure 27 is selected for publication.
claims:
Claims 1. A method for a user equipment, UE, configured to communicate with a wireless network via a master cell group, MCG, and a secondary cell group, SCG, the method comprising: entering (2720) a reduced-energy mode for the SCG responsive to receiving a first command via the MCG or the SCG; and while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for the MCG, performing (2730) SCG measurements and reporting the SCG measurements to the wireless network. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the SCG measurements are reported to the wireless network via one of the following: a physical uplink shared channel, PUSCH, of the MCG; a PUSCH of the SCG; a physical uplink control channel, PUCCH, of the MCG; and a PUCCH of the SCG 3. The method of any of claims 1-2, wherein the SCG measurements are reported in one of the following: a medium access control, MAC, control element, CE, sent via the MCG, a radio resource control, RRC, message sent via the MCG, layer- 1 uplink control information, UCI, sent via the MCG, or layer- 1 UCI sent via the SCG. 4. The method of any of claims 1-3, wherein: the SCG includes a primary SCG cell, PSCell, and one or more secondary cells, SCells; the SCG measurements are reported via the PSCell; and the one or more SCells have no uplinks configured while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein reporting (2730) the SCG measurements while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG is responsive to one of the following: one or more conditions that are also applicable to reporting of SCG measurements while in the connected mode for the SCG; or 

 a reporting period that is greater than a reporting period for SCG measurements while in the connected mode for the SCG. 6. The method of any of claims 1-5, further comprising: while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG, receiving (2740), via the MCG, a TCI state associated with a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, of the SCG; and upon exiting the reduced-energy mode for the SCG, monitoring (2780) the PDCCH of the SCG based on the received TCI state. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the received TCI state is different than a most recent TCI state associated with the PDCCH of the SCG, the most recent TCI state being received before entering the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 8. The method of any of claims 6-7, further comprising receiving (2770) a second command to enter the connected mode for the SCG, wherein exiting the reduced-energy mode for the SCG is responsive to the second command. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein one of the following applies: the TCI state is received via the MCG and the second command is received via the SCG; or the TCI state and the second command are received concurrently via the MCG. 10. The method of any of claims 8-9, wherein: one of the following first conditions applies: the second command is received and the SCG measurements are reported via a same one of the MCG and the SCG; or the second command is received and the SCG measurements are reported via different ones of the MCG and the SCG; and one of the following second conditions applies: the TCI state is received and the SCG measurements are reported via a same one of the MCG and the SCG; or the TCI state is received and the SCG measurements are reported via different ones of the MCG and the SCG. 11. The method of any of claims 6-10, wherein: the TCI state is received as a medium access control, MAC, control element, CE, via a PDCCH in a first cell of the SCG, and the method further comprises performing (2750) one or more of the following while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG: refraining from monitoring PDCCH in one or more other cells of the SCG; and monitoring a subset of the PDCCH in the first cell of the SCG based on the TCI state. 12. The method of any of claims 6-10, wherein: the TCI state is received as a medium access control, MAC, control element, CE, via a PDCCH in a first cell of the MCG; and the method further comprises refraining (2760) from monitoring PDCCH in one or more other cells of the MCG while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 13. The method of any of claims 1-12, further comprising receiving (2710) one of the following while in the connected mode for the SCG: a measurement configuration for the SCG, which indicates the SCG measurements to be performed and reported while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG, or a measurement configuration for the MCG, which indicates SCG measurements to be performed and reported while in the connected mode for the MCG. 14. A method for a second network node configured to provide a secondary cell group, SCG, for a user equipment, UE, in a wireless network, the method comprising: while the UE is in a connected mode for the SCG, sending (2820) to the UE a command to enter a reduced-energy mode for the SCG; and while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for a master cell group, MCG, in the wireless network, receiving (2830) one or more reports of SCG measurements performed by the UE while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein one of the following applies: the reports of SCG measurements are received from a first network node configured to provide the MCG; or 

 the reports of SCG measurements are received from the UE via one of the following: a physical uplink shared channel, PUSCH, of the SCG; or a physical uplink control channel, PUCCH, of the SCG. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the reports of SCG measurements are received from the UE in layer- 1 uplink control information, UCI, via the SCG. 17. The method of any of claims 14-16, wherein: the SCG includes a primary SCG cell, PSCell, and one or more secondary cell, SCells; the reports of SCG measurements are received from the UE via the PSCell; and the one or more SCells have no uplinks configured while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 18. The method of any of claims 14-17, wherein the reports of SCG measurements are received responsive to one of the following: one or more conditions that are also applicable to reporting of SCG measurements while the UE is in the connected mode for the SCG; or a reporting period that is greater than a reporting period for SCG measurements while the UE is in the connected mode for the SCG. 19. The method of any of claims 14-18, further comprising one of the following: sending (2880), to the UE via the SCG, a command to enter the connected mode for the SCG; or sending (2885), to a first network node configured to provide the MCG, a request for the UE to enter the connected mode for the SCG. 20. The method of any of claims 14-19, further comprising: based on the one or more reports of SCG measurements, determining (2850) a TCI state associated with a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, of the SCG; while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG, sending (2860) the TCI state to the UE or to a first network node configured to provide the MCG; and. after the UE exits the reduced-energy mode for the SCG, transmitting (2890) the PDCCH based on the TCI state. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the TCI state is different than a most recent TCI state associated with the PDCCH of the SCG, the most recent TCI state being sent to the UE before the UE entered the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 22. The method of any of claims 20-21, further comprising receiving (2840), from the first network node, a request for an updated TCI state associated with the PDCCH of the SCG, wherein sending (2860) the TCI state to the first network node in responsive to the request. 23. The method of any of claim 20-22, wherein: the TCI state is sent to the UE as a medium access control, MAC, control element, CE, via a PDCCH in a first cell of the SCG; and one or more of the following applies: the method further comprises refraining (2870) from transmitting PDCCH to the UE in one or more other cells of the SCG while the UE is in the reduced- energy mode for the SCG, and the TCI state is sent in a subset of the PDCCH in the first cell of the SCG. 24. The method of any of claims 14-23, further comprising sending (2810), to the UE while the UE is in the connected mode for the SCG, a measurement configuration for the SCG that indicates SCG measurements to be performed and reported while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 25. A method for a first network node configured to provide a master cell group, MCG, for a user equipment, UE, in a wireless network, the method comprising: while the UE is in a connected mode for the MCG and in a reduced-energy for a secondary cell group, SCG, in the wireless network, receiving (2920), from the UE via the MCG, one or more reports of SCG measurements performed by the UE while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG; and sending (2990), to the UE via the MCG, a command to enter the connected mode for the SCG. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the reports of SCG measurements are received from the UE via one of the following: a physical uplink shared channel, PUSCH; or a physical uplink control channel, PUCCH. 27. The method of any of claims 25-26, wherein each report of SCG measurements is received from the UE in one of the following: a medium access control, MAC, control element, CE; a radio resource control, RRC, message, layer- 1 uplink control information, UCI. 28. The method of any of claims 25-27, further comprising forwarding (2930) the received reports of SCG measurements to a second network node configured to provide the SCG. 29. The method of any of embodiments 25-28, further comprising, while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG, sending (2960), to the UE via the MCG, a TCI state associated with a physical downlink control channel, PDCCH, of the SCG. 30. The method of claim 29, further comprising one of the following: receiving (2950) the TCI state from a second network node configured to provide the SCG; or determining (2955) the TCI state based on the received reports of SCG measurements. 31. The method of claim 30, further comprising sending (2940), to the second network node, a request for an updated TCI state associated with the PDCCH of the SCG, wherein receiving (2950) the TCI state from the second network node is responsive to the request. 32. The method of any of claims 29-31, wherein the TCI state and the command are sent concurrently. 33. The method of any of claims 29-32, wherein: the TCI state is sent as a medium access control, MAC, control element, CE, via a PDCCH in a first cell of the MCG; and the method further comprises refraining (2970) from transmitting PDCCH to the UE in one or more other cells of the MCG while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG. 34. The method of any of claims 25-33, further comprising receiving (2980), from a second network node configured to provide the SCG, a request for the UE to enter the connected mode for the SCG, wherein sending (2990) the command is responsive to the request. 35. The method of any of claims 25-34, further comprising sending (2910), to the UE, a measurement configuration for the MCG that indicates SCG measurements to be performed and reported while the UE is in the connected mode for the MCG. 36. A user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) configured to communicate with a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143) via a master cell group, MCG, and a secondary cell group, SCG, the UE comprising: communication interface circuitry (3014, 3109, 3131, 3437) configured to communicate with the wireless network via the SCG and the MCG; and processing circuitry (3020, 3101, 3438) operatively coupled to the communication interface circuitry, whereby the processing circuitry and the communication interface circuitry are configured to: enter a reduced-energy mode for the SCG responsive to receiving a first command via the MCG or the SCG; and while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for the MCG, perform SCG measurements and report the SCG measurements to the wireless network. 37. The UE of claim 36, wherein the processing circuitry and the communication interface circuitry are further configured to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 2-13. 38. A user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) configured to communicate with a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143) via a master cell group, MCG, and a secondary cell group, SCG, the UE being further configured to: enter a reduced-energy mode for the SCG responsive to receiving a first command via the MCG or the SCG; and while in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for the MCG, perform SCG measurements and report the SCG measurements to the wireless network. 39. The UE of claim 38, being further configured to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 2-13. 40. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium (3030, 3121) storing computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry (3020, 3101, 3438) of a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) configured to communicate with a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143) via a master cell group, MCG, and a secondary cell group, SCG, configure the UE to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 1-13. 41. A computer program product (3125, 3431) comprising computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry (3020, 3101, 3438) of a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) configured to communicate with a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143) via a master cell group, MCG, and a secondary cell group, SCG, configure the UE to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 1-13. 42. A second network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1820, 3060, 3230, 3420) arranged to provide a secondary cell group, SCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), the second network node comprising: communication interface circuitry (3090, 3270, 32200, 3426, 3427) configured to communicate with the UE via SCG and with a first network node arranged to provide a master cell group, MCG, for the UE in the wireless network; and processing circuitry (3070, 3260, 3428) operatively coupled to the communication interface circuitry, whereby the processing circuitry and the communication interface circuitry are configured to: while the UE is in a connected mode for the SCG, send to the UE a command to enter a reduced-energy mode for the SCG; and while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for a master cell group, MCG, in the wireless network, receive one or more reports of SCG measurements performed by the UE while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG. 43. The second network node of claim 42, wherein the processing circuitry and the communication interface circuitry are further configured to perform operations corresponding to 

any of the methods of claims 15-24. 44. A second network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1820, 3060, 3230, 3420) arranged to provide a secondary cell group, SCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), the second network node being further configured to: while the UE is in a connected mode for the SCG, send to the UE a command to enter a reduced-energy mode for the SCG; and while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in a connected mode for a master cell group, MCG, in the wireless network, receive one or more reports of SCG measurements performed by the UE while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG. 45. The second network node of claim 44, being further configured to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 15-24. 46. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium (3080, 3290) storing computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry (3070, 3260, 3428) of a second network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1820, 3060, 3230, 3420) configured to provide a secondary cell group, SCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), configure the second network node to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 14-24. 47. A computer program product (3295, 3421) comprising computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry (3070, 3260, 3428) of a second network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1820, 3060, 3230, 3420) configured to provide a secondary cell group, SCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), configure the second network node to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 14-24. 48. A first network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1830, 3060, 3230, 3420) configured to provide a master cell group, MCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), the first network node comprising: communication interface circuitry (3090, 3270, 32200, 3426, 3427) configured to 

 communicate with the UE via the MCG and with a second network node arranged to provide a secondary cell group, SCG, for the UE in the wireless network; and processing circuitry (3070, 3260, 3428) operatively coupled to the communication interface circuitry, whereby the processing circuitry and the communication interface circuitry are configured to: while the UE is in a connected mode for the MCG and in a reduced-energy for a secondary cell group, SCG, in the wireless network, receive from the UE, via the MCG, one or more reports of SCG measurements performed by the UE while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG; and send, to the UE via the MCG, a command to enter the connected mode for the SCG 49. The first network node of claim 48, wherein the processing circuitry and the communication interface circuitry are further configured to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 26-35. 50. A first network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1830, 3060, 3230, 3420) configured to provide a master cell group, MCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), the first network node being further configured to: while the UE is in a connected mode for the MCG and in a reduced-energy for a secondary cell group, SCG, in the wireless network, receive from the UE, via the MCG, one or more reports of SCG measurements performed by the UE while the UE is in the reduced-energy mode for the SCG and in the connected mode for the MCG; and send, to the UE via the MCG, a command to enter the connected mode for the SCG. 51. The first network node of claim 50, being further arranged to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 26-35. 52. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium (3080, 3290) storing computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry (3070, 3260, 3428) of a first network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1830, 3060, 3230, 3420) configured to 

provide a master cell group, MCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), configure the first network node to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 25-35. 53. A computer program product (3295, 3421) comprising computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry (3070, 3260, 3428) of a first network node (105, 110, 115, 1820, 300, 350, 410, 420, 510, 520, 1830, 3060, 3230, 3420) configured to provide a master cell group, MCG, for a user equipment, UE (120, 405, 505, 1810, 3010, 3100, 3430) in a wireless network (100, 399, 499, 599, 3143), configure the first network node to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of claims 25-35.
